remember: Students will be able to recall different types of rights, such as fundamental rights and constitutional rights.
Students will be able to explain the concept of right to equality and non-discrimination.
Students will be able to list the features of the Indian Constitution that protect individual rights.
understand: Students will be able to understand the importance of individual rights in a democratic society.
Students will be able to analyze the role of the judiciary in protecting individual rights.
Students will be able to explain the concept of individual liberty and its relationship with the state.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of right to information to real-life situations.
Students will be able to use the principles of equality and non-discrimination to evaluate social and political issues.
Students will be able to design a plan to promote individual rights and freedoms in a local community.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the Indian Constitution in protecting individual rights.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of international human rights conventions on national laws and policies.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different models of human rights protection in various countries.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1.1: Explain the concept and types of rights.
As per NCERT objective 1.2: Describe the features of the Indian Constitution that protect individual rights.
As per NCERT objective 1.3: Analyze the role of the judiciary in protecting individual rights.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the concept of right to equality and non-discrimination.
Describe the features of the Indian Constitution that protect individual rights.
Analyze the impact of the judiciary on protecting individual rights in India.
